title: Harbor Blvd elevated automated light metro (Fullerton → Santa Ana)
_about: Corridor cost profile for the v3 pipeline BCA (spec 2026-07-14 §2.3). The ONLY BCA-owned prices live here and in the engine RANGES (§4). Quantities come from the ridership-model export; this file supplies prices, the corridor service design, the O&M / capital bands, and (INTERIM, until oc 08-A3.3) the central-kernel designation + the five re-priced prior bands the wrapper must not literalize in source (G-E5).

design._note: spec 04 harbor design: elevated automated light metro, REM-class GoA4, 2-car peak consist, 1.0-mi stations. 12.1-mi corridor (scripts/anchor_from_apc.py, scripts/route43_share.py).
design._stations_note: 1.0-mi spacing over 12.1 mi → 13 stations (12 intervals + endpoints).
design._fleet_note: spec 04 §3.1 illustrative Harbor fleet: 11 trainsets × 2 cars × 1.12 spare ≈ 25 cars (cycle_time = 2·19.47/51.2·1.15 ≈ 52.5 min; ceil(52.5/5) = 11 trainsets at 5-min peak). Owner 2026-07-17 60-mph design decision raised the derived average speed 47→51.2 km/h (oc v_cruise central 80→96.6 km/h), trimming the fleet 27→25 (oc scripts/capcost.py fleet(); oc test_capcost gate; oc results_harbor.json sweep_fleet[5]=25).

capital._derivation: spec 04 §2 linear MECE rate card (2026 US$M): Total = markup × [Fixed + per-km·route_km + per-stn·stations + per-car·cars + special_structures]. LOW = sheet's efficient-agency rates, markup ADDITIVE 1 + 0.10 + 0.10 = 1.20 (N2 review 2026-07-15: xlsx-formula-level confirmation that the sheet's own markup cell composes design% and contingency% additively — E55 = E51×(1+0.10+0.10) = 1654.2×1.20 = 1985.04 exactly on the sheet's own line items; corrects this profile's prior 1.10×1.10 = 1.21 multiplicative misread). US-TYPICAL = soft 17% × contingency 19% = 1.3923 multiplicative, with a 1.75× delivery-environment factor on viaduct+stations (§3.2) and the §3.3b dense-segment utilities/street rates. Both K in $B (engine params.K units: cost_$M = K·share·1000).

low._breakdown: per-km civil (viaduct 23 + track 4 + traction 8.5 + CBTC 4 + utilities 3 + street-restoration 3 = 45.5)×19.47 = 885.9; stations 28.3×13 = 367.9; fixed (OCC 28 + depot 125) = 153; cars 6.2×25 = 155.0 (owner 2026-07-17 60-mph design trims the fleet 27→25); special structures 4×30 = 120; subtotal 1681.8 × 1.20 (additive 1+0.10+0.10, E55-anchored, N2 review 2026-07-15 — was × 1.21 multiplicative) = 2018.16, rounded 2018. $/km 103.6 sits inside the sheet's 100-125 $M/km sanity band (§2).

us_typical._breakdown: per-km (viaduct 23×1.75 + track 4 + traction 8.5 + CBTC 4 + utilities 8 + street 6 = 70.75)×19.47 = 1377.5; stations 28.3×1.75×13 = 643.8; fixed 153; cars 155.0 (owner 2026-07-17 60-mph design trims the fleet 27→25); special structures 4×65 = 260; subtotal 2589.3 × 1.3923 = 3605. $/km 185.1 is an efficient-US-typical elevated (Transit Costs Project US-elevated comps run higher; §3.2 cross-check row).
us_typical._provenance_note: composition not gate-locked; additive-consistent alternative 1.36 (~2.4% lower) — convention choice documented, N2 review 2026-07-15. Kept at the committed 1.3923 multiplicative pending a US-TYPICAL-side xlsx anchor equivalent to LOW's E55.

_assets_note: engine DEFAULT_ASSETS split (civil / track+systems / rolling stock / fare+IT). In-service semantics: capexSchedulePipeline spreads the initial build over build_years and measures renewals + residual from each class's in-service date (spec §5).

service_plan._note: E5 car-km design inputs (spec §7). car_km_yr = eq_days · Σ_periods 2·route_km·(60/headway)·hours·cars_per_train. Central eq_days 300 → car_km_yr ≈ 3.50M car-km/yr (peak 5607 + offpeak 6075 = 11682 car-km/weekday).

om._note: GoA4 O&M benchmark bands (spec §7), WIDE, anchored to SkyTrain / Copenhagen Metro / NTD GoA4 automated-metro systems. goaOM(t) = om_fixed_yr + om_var_per_car_km·car_km_yr ($/yr, strictly fiscal). At central: 30M + 4.5×3.50M = 45.8M/yr. om_lo/om_hi rows sweep both endpoints together.

traction_gco2_per_km._note: D8 traction-power carbon debit central = corridor grid rate (SCE). PHYSICAL basis: SCE grid ~200-220 gCO2/kWh × light-metro net traction ~3-4 kWh/car-km ≈ ~700 g/car-km. THE ENGINE RANGES CLAMP traction_gco2_per_km[0,60] (test-locked) BINDS: the effective rate is capped at 60. The term is immaterial to the headline (≈ (55/1e6)·3.5e6·50 = $9.6k/yr vs $2-3.6B capex), so the clamp does not affect results. Central set to 55 (in-band, visible); traction_0 clean-grid zero is the sensitivity. Units tension flagged to the engine owner (T3 report).

avoidable_rate._note: $/rev-hr, spec §6: marginal → fully-allocated. NTD fully-allocated bus O&M ~$150-200/rev-hr overstates what folding two routes sheds, so central 120 sits below it; avoidable_marginal row = 70 (marginal end).

avg_fare._note: Effective system revenue per boarding, transfer/pass-discounted. OCTA base fare $2.00 (oc registry default_fare) × ~0.45-0.80 effective (free transfers, day/month passes, senior/disabled discounts). fareRevDay = avg_fare·(total − base_boardings), net-new boardings only (D3).

_base_boardings_note: Existing 43+543 corridor weekday boardings the new line captures (they already pay the flat fare; only NET-NEW boardings add system revenue — D3, gross overstates ~3.3×). Anchor central: config anchor_low/high 7650/9650 → midpoint 8650.

seat_capacity._note: loadFlag denominator is peak-HOUR seated capacity in the peak direction (seats/hr): 2-car × 150 seats = 300 seats/train × 12 trains/hr (5-min peak headway) = 3600 seats/hr. Central load ≈ R0·0.17·0.60/3600 ≈ 0.32 — below comfort, flag does not fire (D4).

_peak_note: D4 BCA-owned peak-HOUR share (0.17) × peak-direction 0.60 — NOT the export's stage-2 period pkshare U(0.45,0.60), which would overstate hourly load ~3× and fire the flag spuriously (spec §3).
_central_kernel_note: INTERIM (spec §2, until oc 08-A3.3): the central-kernel designation is declared here because the export ships the abc_weights label list but does NOT mark which is central. The wrapper resolves the ABC column against this label — never a hardcoded string in wrapper source (G-E5). R1 launch-equivalent central. TODO: migrate to assumptions.json 08-A3.3 `central` flag.
_abc_rows_note: INTERIM (G-E5): the σ-sensitivity ABC tornado rows map to the export's s350/s800 kernels. The kernel LABELS live here (not literalized in wrapper source, same rule as central_kernel); the row IDs (abc_s350/abc_s800) are spec §10 ids the wrapper may carry. TODO: migrate to 08-A3.3.

prior_bands._note: INTERIM (spec §2, until oc 08-A3.3): (lo,hi,shape) endpoints for the five priors the wrapper RE-PRICES. The export's params arrays are the per-draw SAMPLED values (empirical percentiles), not the band endpoints the pcar_*/vot_behav_* rows require, so the endpoints live here. TODO: migrate to assumptions.json 08-A3.3 machine values section; G-E5's grep gate activates there.

central_profile._note: The named central profile (spec §3, spec 06 presentation principle). λ=1, γ=0, SCC=$50, mohring=0, labor=0 (explicit — NOT left to V2_DEFAULTS 0.18/0.05; G-E3), rebound=0.4, carbon_growth=0, VOT=$22.50, discount 4% flat, ramp_start 0.8, ramp_years 5, growth 0, horizon 60. eq_days is read from the EXPORT (central = min of its (300,330) band), NOT from here. traction_gco2_per_km / peak shares / build_years / O&M / avoidable_rate / avg_fare are the corridor fields above.
central_profile._rebound_note: Central recentered 0 → 0.4 (external review 2026-07-17 + Duranton & Turner induced-refill evidence: freed road capacity is partially re-consumed, so the congestion-relief slice of the avoided-car-mile credit is haircut at central, not only in a row). Sensitivity rows at 0 (rebound_0, the old no-refill central) and 0.8 (rebound_hi). Materiality: the car-mile externality slice is ~4% of user benefits, so the recenter moves the headline only slightly.
central_profile._ext_note: c_cong/c_acc/c_emis_local centrals match the engine pipeline defaults and the endogenous CONST (0.20 / 0.03 / local-pollutant 0.0085, D8 re-spec 0.7-1.0¢/mi). Swept by ext_cong/acc/local rows.
